assistant_output_excerpt:
Validating phase 2 in order: local boot, then Docker build, then Docker Compose.
Running local boot check with secrets sourced but not printed.
Local boot passed. Moving to Docker build.
Docker build passed. Moving to Docker Compose.
Compose is up and serving. I’m fixing the stale default model pin to the current Claude Sonnet slug before the final validation pass.
Model pin updated. Re-running tests and style checks before rebuilding containers.
Tests and RuboCop passed. Rebuilding Docker image and Compose with the corrected model default.
Docker and Compose passed after the model update. Running one RubyLLM smoke check with the sourced OpenRouter environment, without printing any secret or response content.
Cleaning up containers and generated local artifacts, then scanning the project for accidental secret material.
The first scan flagged safe environment variable references, not values. Re-running for actual key material only and confirming final state.
Phase 2 complete.

Validated and fixed in place:

- Local Rails app boots and responds on `/up` and `/`
- Updated default chat model to `anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.6`
- RubyLLM OpenRouter smoke test passed with `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` sourced from `~/.config/zsh/secrets`
- `bin/rails test` passes: 18 tests, 83 assertions
- `bundle exec rubocop` passes with no offenses
- `docker build -t rubyllm-chat-phase2 .` succeeds
- `docker compose up --build -d` succeeds and serves `/up` and `/`
- Compose stack was stopped afterward
- Removed generated `config/master.key`, coverage output, and local boot logs
- Scanned project files for OpenRouter key material; none found

No secrets were written into project files, logs, Dockerfile, compose config, or README.
command:
Once finished coding and testing, you must make sure it runs, both locally, and that docker build also finishes successfully, and docker compose is able to run.

Do not ask for confirmation. Do not stop with a plan. Fulfill the request and finish.

This is phase 2 of 2 and a continuation of the existing project in the current working directory. Do not regenerate the app. Do not create a second app. Validate and fix the current codebase in place.

Use environment variables so RubyLLM actually works. Source ~/.config/zsh/secrets only to load the required environment, especially OPENROUTER_API_KEY. Do not print secret values, do not copy secret file contents into the repo, and do not commit secrets into files, logs, Dockerfiles, compose files, or README examples.

Validate the project end-to-end in this order:
- run the local app in a way that proves it boots
- make any fixes needed so it boots cleanly
- make sure `docker build` finishes successfully
- make sure `docker compose up --build` is able to run

If one step passes, move to the next without asking for confirmation. If something fails, fix it and continue. Only stop when you have actually completed the validation work or you hit a hard blocker you cannot resolve from inside the workspace.

model.label: Gemini 3.5 Flash
model.selection_reason: Google Gemini 3.5 Flash on OpenRouter (GA snapshot gemini-3.5-flash-20260519). Added 2026-06-15 to run + score in-house after community PR #6 submitted pre-scored results without the gitignored project code. Input $1.50/M, output $9.00/M, 1M context. Tests whether a Flash-tier model can match the 3.1 Pro RubyLLM correctness baseline.

project_summary.works_note: Rails app, tests, README, and container files detected.
